questions.question: Why would completely restricting access to frontier AI be self-defeating for America?
options: Most AI companies are based in Europe
American AI firms depend heavily on international customers and talent
questions.explanation: The article notes that companies such as Anthropic rely on overseas users, researchers and revenue.

questions.question: What hierarchy of access does the article predict?
options: Everyone will receive identical AI models
Different countries may receive AI systems of different capability
China will receive the best models
questions.explanation: The most advanced systems may remain restricted while weaker versions are exported.

questions.question: Why might America's allies feel uncomfortable?
options: Because AI could become another source of American political influence
Because Europe has more computing power
questions.explanation: Dependence on American AI could strengthen Washington's bargaining power.

questions.question: According to the article, what alternative might some countries choose?
questions.explanation: Countries may prefer open-weight models or competitors if American restrictions become too severe.

questions.question: Which factor gives America another major advantage besides AI models?
questions.explanation: The article argues that America also dominates compute infrastructure.
